head.hi: Memorandum of Conversation, by Mr. John Foster Dulles of the United States Delegation

div.p: I spoke to Ambassador Chauvel at 11:00 a. m. in the Delegates’ Lounge today. Chauvel said that he had expected this matter to be discussed by M. Schuman with Mr. Acheson, but they had not gotten to it.That is, in the September 18–19 pre-General Assembly meetings of the Secretary of State with the British and French Foreign Ministers. France was not favorably disposed. They felt our proposal was unconstitutional, usurping Security Council authority.

list.item: He accepted the 24 hour special Assembly on call of 7 members of the Security Council, but not of a majority of the Members.
He saw no particular difficulty in Part B.

He doubted the whole idea of C, as being a matter to be dealt with by the Security Council under Article 43. Also, practically, if it was hard to create a collective force for 12, could it be done for 59 nations?

div.p: He would translate our text, study it and discuss further in a few days. Meanwhile, he would like a memorandum from us, Infra. which I offered on the constitutional question.
